briefdescription.para: The main entry point for the EmacLite driver example in interrupt mode.

detaileddescription.para: This function will transmit/receive the Ethernet frames and verify the data in the received frame (if the MDIO interface is configured in the EmacLite core). This function simply transmits a frame if the MDIO interface is not configured in the EmacLite core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the EmacLite.
contains the base address of the EmacLite device

briefdescription.para: This function sends a frame of given size.

detaileddescription.para: This function assumes interrupt mode and sends the frame.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the EmacLite instance.
is the size of the frame to create. The size only reflects the payload size, it does not include the Ethernet header size (14 bytes) nor the Ethernet CRC size (4 bytes).

briefdescription.para: This function receives a frame of given size.

detaileddescription.para: This function assumes interrupt mode, receives the frame and verifies its contents.

parameterdescription.para: is the size of the frame to receive. The size only reflects the payload size, it does not include the Ethernet header size (14 bytes) nor the Ethernet CRC size (4 bytes).

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, a driver-specific return code if not.

briefdescription.para: This function handles the receive callback from the EmacLite driver.

parameterdescription.para: is the call back reference provided to the Handler.

briefdescription.para: This function handles the transmit callback from the EmacLite driver.

parameterdescription.para: is the call back reference provided to the Handler.

briefdescription.para: This function is the main function of the EmacLite interrupt example.
This file contains an example for using the EmacLite hardware and driver.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains an interrupt example outlining the use of interrupts and callbacks in the transmission/reception of an Ethernet frame of 1000 bytes of payload.

If the MDIO interface is NOT configured in the EmacLite core then this example will transmit a frame. If the MDIO interface is configured in the EmacLite core then this example will enable the MAC loopback in the PHY device, then transmit the frame and compare the received frame.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.01a ecm 05/21/04 First release 1.01a sv 06/06/05 Minor changes to comply to Doxygen and coding guidelines 1.01a sv 06/06/06 Minor changes for supporting Test App Interrupt examples 2.00a ktn 02/25/09 Updated to use PHY loop back if MDIO is configured in core 3.00a ktn 10/22/09 Updated the example to use the HAL APIs/macros. Updated example to use the macros that have been changed in the driver to remove _m from the name of the macro. 3.01a ktn 07/08/10 Updated example to support Little Endian MicroBlaze. 4.2 adk 29/02/16 Updated example to support Zynq and ZynqMP. 4.3 ms 01/23/17 Added xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028.
